criteria.name: AC coverage
criteria.description: Cross-reference PRD acceptance criteria against test plan test cases
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Cross-reference PRD `- [ ]` items against test plan test cases — report coverage %

criteria.name: Test framework specified
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for framework/tooling section (e.g., Jest, Playwright, pytest)

criteria.name: Story-to-test mapping
criteria.description: Verify each PRD story has a corresponding test section
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify each PRD story has a corresponding test section

criteria.name: Error scenarios present
criteria.description: Check for negative/error test cases per story
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for negative/error test cases per story

criteria.name: Boundary conditions tested
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for boundary value tests (empty inputs, max values, off-by-one, null/undefined)

criteria.name: Failure modes covered
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for failure mode tests (network errors, invalid data, timeouts, permission denied)

criteria.name: Integration points mapped
criteria.description: Check for integration test cases covering component interactions
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for integration test cases covering interactions between components, epics, or stories

criteria.name: Component interactions verified
criteria.description: Check that integration tests cover data flow between components
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check that integration tests cover data flow between components (not just individual units)

criteria.name: Data flow boundaries tested
criteria.description: Check for tests at data transformation points
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for tests at data transformation points (input parsing, output formatting, cross-system boundaries)

criteria.name: E2E scenarios cover critical journeys
criteria.description: Check for end-to-end test cases mapping to primary user workflows
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Check for end-to-end test cases mapping to the PRD's primary user workflows

criteria.name: E2E happy paths and error paths
criteria.description: Verify E2E section includes both success and failure scenarios
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Verify E2E section includes both success and failure scenarios

criteria.name: E2E scenarios map to PRD requirements
criteria.description: Cross-reference E2E test cases against PRD requirements for traceability
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Cross-reference E2E test cases against PRD requirements for traceability

criteria.name: Framework consistent with test strategy
criteria.description: Verify test plan aligns with project test strategy
criteria.autoCheckMethod: If `Inception/Test-Strategy.md` exists, verify test plan aligns with its guidance

criteria.name: Coverage targets realistic
criteria.description: Compare stated coverage targets to project scope
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Compare stated coverage targets to project scope — flag unrealistically high (100%) or low (<60%) targets

criteria.name: Test coverage proportionate
criteria.description: Assess whether test plan depth is proportionate to PRD scope
criteria.autoCheckMethod: Assess whether the test plan's depth is proportionate to the PRD's scope. For each PRD story that adds or modifies code paths, verify the test plan includes tests at the appropriate level (unit for logic, integration for component interaction, E2E for user journeys). Flag stories with complex scope (multiple files, refactoring, new modules) that only have shallow test coverage (e.g., only 'existing tests pass' assertions).

subjective.name: Edge cases thorough
subjective.question: Are the edge cases and error scenarios thorough enough for the project's risk profile?

options.label: Thorough
options.description: Error scenarios, boundary conditions, and failure modes well covered

options.label: Minor gaps
options.description: Most error cases covered but some stories missing boundary tests

options.label: Significant gaps
options.description: Many stories lack error scenarios or failure mode testing

subjective.partialReviewNote: Partial reviews are valid — if user declines to answer, record as '⊘ Skipped' and continue.

subjective.name: Strategy appropriate
subjective.question: Is the overall test strategy appropriate for this project's scope and complexity?

options.label: Appropriate
options.description: Coverage targets realistic, test level balance makes sense for the scope

options.label: Needs refinement
options.description: Strategy exists but coverage targets or level balance could improve

options.label: Inappropriate
options.description: Strategy misaligned with project scope or missing key considerations

subjective.partialReviewNote: Partial reviews are valid — if user declines to answer, record as '⊘ Skipped' and continue.

coverageAnalysis.description: P0 coverage analysis procedure — cross-reference PRD acceptance criteria against test plan test cases
procedure: Extract all acceptance criteria from every user story in the PRD (all `- [ ]` items)
For each acceptance criterion, verify a corresponding test case exists in the test plan
Report coverage status for each story: Full coverage (all ACs have test cases), Partial coverage (some ACs missing test cases), No coverage (story has no test cases at all)

_meta.note: Test plan review criteria for /review-test-plan. Extracted from inline command spec table and AskUserQuestion blocks.
